source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

rooms.name: Nest
rooms.windows: opens to Relax via large glass door; additional exterior windows along living area

rooms.windows: single exterior window on upper wall

rooms.name: Dine
rooms.windows: bay/windowed dining nook with multiple panels on exterior wall

rooms.name: Taste
rooms.windows: no prominent exterior window shown; indirect light from adjacent dining

rooms.name: Revive
rooms.windows: small window near tub (if present) otherwise none

rooms.name: Relax
rooms.windows: outdoor/covered patio area with wide openings to living/dining

vibe_report.summary: A warm, restrained Japandi approach that balances Scandinavian lightness with Japanese restraint to create a calm, family-friendly home. Soft, warm neutrals, layered natural textures, and simple, low-profile furniture compose spaces that feel meditative yet practical — with a compact, comfortable work corner and a safe, serene sleep area for a young child.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Use an off-white base (#EDE7DF) to keep volume and reflect light, warmed with mid-tone oak and honey woods (#CBB698 / #A88461). Ground the scheme with soft gray-browns (#6D6A5F) and an espresso accent (#2B2724). Textiles and ceramics echo these tones to maintain low-contrast, tactile depth.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight with warm (2700–3000K) accent sources. Prioritize diffuse window light (golden-hour warmth) and low-hanging woven pendants or paper lanterns for intimate zones. Use dimmable task lighting at the desk and warm strip under kitchen cabinets for function without glare.
materials: light oak (tables, low sofa base, shelving)
woven rattan and seagrass (pendant, baskets, chair accents)
soft-wool and natural-fibre rugs (wool / tatami blend)
blackened steel (slim desk frame, lamp accents)
textures: smooth plaster or matte paint walls
furniture_language: multi-functional small-scale pieces (bench that doubles as storage)
clean, exposed joinery and minimal ornament
styling_rules: Keep surfaces largely decluttered: 1–3 curated objects per visible surface.
Layer textiles (rug + mat + cushion) to add warmth without color contrast.
Center compositions around natural materials — wood, ceramic, woven fiber.
Use plants sparingly for organic color and scale; choose architectural shapes (ficus, small palms, bonsai).
Scale everything to human height — prioritize comfortable sightlines and clear circulation for family use.
avoid: high-gloss finishes, chrome or bright metallics
excessive pattern mixing or saturated colors
overstuffed rooms or heavy, tall furniture that blocks sightlines
visible logos, modern tech clutter, bright task lights without warm diffusion
too many small decor items — avoid busy tabletops

room_guidance.headline: Low, layered family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Compose a low oak-framed sofa facing the patio with a low coffee table and a textured wool/tatami rug. Place the standing desk in a dedicated corner near the patio glass so the parent can work with natural light and sightlines to the outdoor area. Keep circulation from entry to patio clear and keep décor minimal but tactile.
must_include: low neutral sofa on wooden base
compact standing desk with slim black frame
must_avoid: bulky tall bookcases that interrupt flow
bright, saturated throw pillows
cluttered children's toys left visible (keep stored)

room_guidance.headline: Serene parent-and-baby bedroom with safe crib corner
room_guidance.guidance: Arrange a low-profile bed centered on the longest wall and reserve a calm corner for a minimalist crib near the exterior window for natural light. Use soft linen textiles and a small bedside lamp for warm night light. Provide a narrow low dresser or storage bench for baby essentials — keep visual weight low and horizontal.
must_include: small natural-fabric crib placed away from direct sun
soft linen bedding in warm neutrals
rounded ceramic vase or sculptural object
must_avoid: bright mobile or excessive baby decals
cluttered shelving next to crib
overhead harsh lighting

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Choose a compact round oak table (approx. 42–48 in) centered under a woven rattan pendant. Use 3–4 light-upholstered chairs in linen tones. Stage with a single stoneware vase and minimal place settings. Keep approach to patio and living unobstructed.
must_include: rattan woven pendant light centered above table
must_avoid: large rectangular table that dominates the nook
bright patterned placemats or heavy centerpiece
overly ornate chandelier

room_guidance.headline: Minimal, functional kitchen with warm accents
room_guidance.guidance: Keep visible kitchen surfaces clear and warm: matte cabinetry in soft neutral, oak open shelving with curated ceramics, and under-cabinet warm strip lighting. Show modest prep items (olive oil, wooden cutting board) to suggest daily use without clutter.
must_include: open oak shelving with curated ceramics
wooden cutting board and matte ceramic oil cruet
must_avoid: stacked appliances on counters
bright cabinet colors or reflective finishes

room_guidance.headline: Covered patio extension for indoor-outdoor living
room_guidance.guidance: Stage with compact outdoor seating (low wooden bench + cushions) and woven rugs for continuity. Use potted plants and a small teak side table. Ensure visual continuity with living/dining materials and keep furnishings light to emphasize openness.
must_include: low wooden bench with neutral cushions
must_avoid: heavy patio furniture or bright synthetic cushions
too many small decorative items

warnings: Measured dimensions are only available for Nest, Dream, and Dine; other room proportions are inferred from the plan graphic — verify furniture scale before final staging.
Place crib away from direct window glare and cords; ensure real-world safety compliance if used for real clients.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Warm, quiet, family-focused Japandi: light-filled, tactile, and deliberately minimal.
global_style_notes: Favor horizontal lines and low-profile furniture to maximize perceived space.
Limit palette to 3–4 close neutrals with wood as the primary warm anchor.
Use dimmable, warm light sources and layered lighting for evening scenes.
Stage for real family use: a small work corner, organized toy storage out of sight, and a calm crib area.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: avoid neon colors, glossy chrome, busy patterns, visible brand logos, excessive clutter or toys, unrealistic reflections, exaggerated perspective distortion, incorrect furniture scale, harsh cool lighting, modern plastic outdoor furniture

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A warm, restrained Japandi approach that balances Scandinavian lightness with Japanese restraint to create a calm, family-friendly home. Soft, warm neutrals, layered natural textures, and simple, low-profile furniture compose spaces that feel meditative yet practical — with a compact, comfortable work corner and a safe, serene sleep area for a young child.

annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: Warm, quiet, family-focused Japandi: light-filled, tactile, and deliberately minimal.

warnings.message: Double-check measured dimensions for furniture fit in Dine (42–48 in round table recommended to fit 7.83 ft × 8 ft space) and confirm door swing/clearances from the floor plan prior to final asset placement.
Crib placement must comply with safety clearances and should not be placed directly under active pendant lights or within reach of cords.
Measured dimensions are only available for Nest, Dream, and Dine; other room proportions are inferred from the plan graphic — verify furniture scale before final staging.
Place crib away from direct window glare and cords; ensure real-world safety compliance if used for real clients.
